abstract: Education serves as a vital pillar in ensuring the continuity and progress of a nation. However, increasing instances of juvenile delinquency—including physical altercations, drug abuse, and other moral lapses—signal a troubling trend of moral degradation. To combat this, schools must prioritize character building and personality development rooted in religious values. This study explores the "refraction method" (habituation and modeling) as a critical tool for instilling positive traits in children. When the school environment and social surroundings align with Islamic educational teachings, students’ character values flourish. Specifically, this research investigates the implementation of the refraction method at SDN Babakan Sirna Sukabumi. The study addresses four key areas: (1) the primary objectives of using the refraction method for character building; (2) the specific forms and practices of refraction utilized within the school; (3) the evaluation systems in place and the resulting character traits observed in students; and (4) the facilitating factors and obstacles encountered in fostering these Islamic values.
